name: Peer Discovery
description: ES6 module `static/js/discovery.js` implementing peer discovery via bootstrap nodes, peer exchange protocol, self-healing, and peer rotation. Provides a KnownPeersCache class for stateful peer tracking, pure functions for composing/handling peer exchange protocol messages (sent as plain JSON text frames, outside the padded/signed message pipeline), and an initializeDiscovery function that manages periodic health-check and rotation timers. Peer exchange messages are a separate protocol layer — NOT padded, TTL-stamped, or signed.

types.name: int
types.description: Integer number (JavaScript number used as integer)

types.name: float
types.description: Floating point number (JavaScript number)

types.name: str
types.description: JavaScript string

types.name: bool
types.description: JavaScript boolean

types.name: None
types.description: JavaScript undefined or null (function returns nothing meaningful)

types.name: any
types.description: Any JavaScript value

types.name: PeerEntry

fields.name: url
fields.description: WebSocket URL of the peer (e.g., 'wss://example.com/ws')

fields.name: lastSeen
fields.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds (from Date.now()) of when this peer was last seen or added/updated

fields.name: lastLatency
fields.description: Last measured round-trip latency in milliseconds, or null if not yet measured

types.description: A single peer record stored in KnownPeersCache. Represents a known peer with its URL, last-seen timestamp, and optional latency measurement.

types.name: OptionalFloat
types.description: A float value or null

types.name: OptionalStr
types.description: A string value or null

types.name: OptionalPeerEntry
types.description: A PeerEntry or undefined

types.name: PeerEntryList
types.description: Array of PeerEntry objects

types.name: StrList
types.description: Array of strings

types.name: PeerMetadata

fields.name: lastLatency
fields.description: Measured latency in ms, or null/undefined to keep existing or default to null

types.description: Optional metadata provided when adding a peer to the cache. All fields are optional; missing fields use defaults.

types.name: PeerIdentity

fields.name: pubKey
fields.description: Base64-encoded public key of the sender

types.description: Identity block included in peer exchange messages, containing the sender's public key.

types.name: PeerExchangeRequest

fields.name: type
fields.description: Always 'peer_exchange_request'

fields.name: from
fields.description: Identity of the requesting peer

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when the request was created (Date.now())

types.description: A peer exchange request message. Sent as plain JSON text frame over WebSocket.

types.name: PeerExchangeResponse

fields.name: type
fields.description: Always 'peer_exchange_response'

fields.name: peers
fields.description: Array of peer WebSocket URLs to share (max 5)

validators.error_message: peers array must contain 0 to 5 URLs

fields.name: from
fields.description: Identity of the responding peer

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when the response was created (Date.now())

types.description: A peer exchange response message containing a list of known peer URLs. Sent as plain JSON text frame over WebSocket.

types.name: PeerExchangeMessage
types.description: Either a peer exchange request or response

types.name: ConnectedPeerInfo

fields.name: url
fields.description: WebSocket URL of the connected peer

fields.name: connectedAt
fields.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when this peer connection was established

types.description: Information about a currently connected peer, including when the connection was established. Used by selectPeerForRotation.

types.name: ConnectedPeerInfoList
types.description: Array of ConnectedPeerInfo objects

types.name: IsProtocolResult

fields.name: isProtocol
fields.description: True if the data is a valid peer exchange protocol message

fields.name: parsed
fields.description: The parsed JSON object if isProtocol is true, null otherwise

types.description: Result of checking whether raw WebSocket data is a peer exchange protocol message.

types.name: OptionalParsedMessage
types.description: A parsed peer exchange message object or null

types.name: NetworkApi

fields.name: getConnectedPeerCount
fields.description: Function () => int — returns current number of connected peers

fields.name: getConnectedPeerUrls
fields.description: Function () => StrList — returns URLs of all currently connected peers

fields.name: connectToPeer
fields.description: Function (url: str) => void — initiates a WebSocket connection to the given peer URL

fields.name: disconnectPeer
fields.description: Function (url: str) => void — closes the WebSocket connection to the given peer URL

fields.name: sendToPeer
fields.description: Function (url: str, data: str) => void — sends a text frame to a specific connected peer

fields.name: broadcastRaw
fields.description: Function (data: str) => void — broadcasts a text frame to all connected peers

types.description: Interface object providing network operations to initializeDiscovery. Passed by the caller to decouple discovery logic from WebSocket implementation.

types.name: CleanupFunction
types.description: A no-argument function that clears all timers started by initializeDiscovery. Calling it multiple times is safe.

types.name: KnownPeersCache

fields.name: maxSize
fields.description: Maximum number of entries the cache can hold. Set at construction time.

validators.error_message: maxSize must be a positive integer

types.description: Class managing a bounded set of known peers with LRU-style eviction based on lastSeen timestamp. Uses an internal Map<string, PeerEntry>. Acceptable as a class per SOPs because it manages stateful lifecycle.

functions.name: KnownPeersCache.constructor
functions.description: Creates a new KnownPeersCache instance with the given maximum capacity. Initializes an empty internal Map.

inputs.name: maxSize
inputs.description: Maximum number of peer entries to store. Defaults to KNOWN_PEERS_MAX (50).

validators.error_message: maxSize must be a positive integer between 1 and 10000

postconditions: Returned cache has size() === 0
Returned cache has maxSize set to the provided value or 50

functions.name: KnownPeersCache.add
functions.description: Adds a new peer entry or updates an existing one. Sets lastSeen to Date.now() on every call. If the cache is at maxSize and the URL is new, evicts the entry with the lowest (oldest) lastSeen value before inserting the new entry.

inputs.name: url
inputs.description: WebSocket URL of the peer to add or update

validators.error_message: url must be a non-empty string

inputs.name: metadata
inputs.description: Optional metadata to merge into the peer entry

error_cases.name: invalid_url
error_cases.condition: url is not a non-empty string

postconditions: cache.get(url) returns a PeerEntry with lastSeen approximately equal to Date.now()
cache.size() <= maxSize
If url was new and cache was at maxSize, the entry with the lowest lastSeen was evicted

structured_side_effects.description: Inserts or updates a PeerEntry, may evict oldest entry

functions.name: KnownPeersCache.get
functions.description: Returns the PeerEntry for the given URL, or undefined if not found. Does NOT update lastSeen (read-only operation).
postconditions: If URL exists in cache, returns the corresponding PeerEntry
If URL does not exist, returns undefined
Cache state is not modified

functions.name: KnownPeersCache.getAll
functions.description: Returns a shallow copy array of all PeerEntry objects currently in the cache.
postconditions: Returned array length equals cache.size()
Returned array is a new array (not the internal data structure)
Each element is a PeerEntry with url, lastSeen, and lastLatency fields

functions.name: KnownPeersCache.remove
functions.description: Removes the peer entry for the given URL from the cache. No-op if the URL is not present.

inputs.name: url
inputs.description: WebSocket URL of the peer to remove

postconditions: cache.get(url) returns undefined after removal
cache.size() decreased by 1 if url was present, unchanged otherwise

structured_side_effects.description: Deletes the entry for the given URL if it exists

functions.name: KnownPeersCache.size
functions.description: Returns the current number of entries in the cache.
postconditions: Returned value is >= 0 and <= maxSize

functions.name: getBootstrapNodes
functions.description: Returns a hardcoded array of bootstrap WebSocket URLs. For Phase 1, derives a single WebSocket URL from the provided origin (or window.location.origin). Converts 'https://' to 'wss://' and 'http://' to 'ws://', appending '/ws' path. Accepts optional origin parameter for testability.

inputs.name: origin
inputs.description: The origin URL to derive the WebSocket bootstrap URL from. Defaults to window.location.origin in browser context.

error_cases.name: missing_origin
error_cases.condition: origin is undefined/null AND window.location is not available

error_data.message: Cannot determine origin: no origin parameter and window.location is unavailable

postconditions: Returned array has at least 1 element
Each element is a valid WebSocket URL string starting with 'ws://' or 'wss://'

structured_side_effects.description: Reads browser global window.location.origin if no origin parameter is provided

functions.name: createPeerExchangeRequest
functions.description: Creates a peer exchange request message object. The message is a plain JSON-serializable object intended to be sent as a text frame.

inputs.name: pubKey
inputs.description: Base64-encoded public key of the requesting peer

validators.error_message: pubKey must be a non-empty string

postconditions: Returned object has type === 'peer_exchange_request'
Returned object has from.pubKey === pubKey
Returned object has timestamp approximately equal to Date.now()

functions.name: createPeerExchangeResponse
functions.description: Creates a peer exchange response message object containing a list of known peer URLs. The message is a plain JSON-serializable object intended to be sent as a text frame.

inputs.name: peerUrls
inputs.description: Array of peer WebSocket URLs to include in the response

inputs.name: pubKey
inputs.description: Base64-encoded public key of the responding peer

validators.error_message: pubKey must be a non-empty string

preconditions: peerUrls is an array of strings
postconditions: Returned object has type === 'peer_exchange_response'
Returned object has peers array equal to peerUrls
Returned object has from.pubKey === pubKey
Returned object has timestamp approximately equal to Date.now()

functions.name: handlePeerExchangeRequest
functions.description: Handles an incoming peer exchange request by producing a peer exchange response. Returns up to 5 connected peer URLs, excluding the requester's URL. Uses the requesting peer's pubKey as the 'from' identity in the response would be wrong — uses the local peer's list of connected URLs instead. The caller is responsible for sending the response.

inputs.name: message
inputs.description: The incoming peer exchange request message (already parsed)

inputs.name: connectedPeerUrls
inputs.description: Array of URLs of all currently connected peers (from networkApi.getConnectedPeerUrls)

inputs.name: requesterUrl
inputs.description: The WebSocket URL of the peer that sent the request (used to exclude from response)

error_cases.name: invalid_message
error_cases.condition: message is null, undefined, or missing type field

preconditions: message is a valid PeerExchangeRequest object
connectedPeerUrls is an array of strings
postconditions: Returned array has length <= 5
Returned array does not contain requesterUrl
All returned URLs are from connectedPeerUrls

functions.name: handlePeerExchangeResponse
functions.description: Handles an incoming peer exchange response by adding each received peer URL to the KnownPeersCache. Each URL is added with default metadata (lastLatency: null).

inputs.name: message
inputs.description: The incoming peer exchange response message (already parsed)

inputs.name: cache
inputs.description: The KnownPeersCache instance to add discovered peers to

error_cases.name: invalid_message
error_cases.condition: message is null, undefined, or message.peers is not an array

error_cases.name: invalid_cache
error_cases.condition: cache is null, undefined, or not a KnownPeersCache instance

preconditions: message is a valid PeerExchangeResponse object with a peers array
postconditions: Each URL in message.peers has been added to cache (cache.get(url) is defined for each)
Cache size does not exceed maxSize (eviction may have occurred)

structured_side_effects.description: Adds each peer URL from the response to the cache

functions.name: selectPeerForRotation
functions.description: Selects the peer that has been connected the longest (lowest connectedAt timestamp) for rotation/disconnection. Returns the URL of that peer, or null if the list is empty.

inputs.name: connectedPeers
inputs.description: Array of currently connected peers with their connection timestamps

error_cases.name: empty_list
error_cases.condition: connectedPeers is empty or not an array

postconditions: If connectedPeers is non-empty, returned URL is the one with the lowest connectedAt value
If connectedPeers is empty, returns null
Returned URL (if not null) exists in the connectedPeers array

functions.name: selectPeerForConnection
functions.description: Selects a random peer URL from the cache that is not already in the connected set. Uses crypto.getRandomValues() for secure random index selection. Returns null if no eligible peers are available.

inputs.name: connectedUrls
inputs.description: Array of currently connected peer URLs to exclude from selection

error_cases.name: no_eligible_peers
error_cases.condition: All cached peer URLs are in connectedUrls, or cache is empty

preconditions: connectedUrls is an array of strings
postconditions: If returned value is not null, it exists in cache.getAll() but not in connectedUrls
If all cached peers are already connected, returns null
If cache is empty, returns null

functions.name: isProtocolMessage
functions.description: Checks if raw WebSocket data is a peer exchange protocol message. First checks if data is a string (binary frames are not protocol messages). Then attempts JSON.parse in a try/catch. If parsed successfully, checks if the 'type' property is a string starting with 'peer_exchange_'. Returns an object with isProtocol boolean and the parsed message (or null).

inputs.name: data
inputs.description: Raw WebSocket message data — could be a string (text frame) or ArrayBuffer/Uint8Array (binary frame)

error_cases.name: non_string_data
error_cases.condition: data is not a string (e.g., ArrayBuffer, Uint8Array, number)

error_cases.name: invalid_json
error_cases.condition: data is a string but not valid JSON

error_cases.name: non_protocol_json
error_cases.condition: data is valid JSON but type field does not start with 'peer_exchange_'

postconditions: If isProtocol is true, parsed is a non-null object with a type field starting with 'peer_exchange_'
If isProtocol is false, parsed is null
Function never throws — all parse errors are caught internally

functions.name: initializeDiscovery
functions.description: Starts periodic peer discovery and rotation timers. Sets up a DISCOVERY_INTERVAL (30s) health check that: (1) checks if connected peer count is below MIN_PEERS, (2) if so, attempts to connect to a peer from the cache or bootstrap nodes, and (3) broadcasts peer exchange requests. Sets up a ROTATION_INTERVAL (5min) rotation that: (1) selects the longest-connected peer for disconnection, (2) disconnects it, and (3) connects a new peer from cache. Returns a cleanup function that clears both intervals. The cleanup function is safe to call multiple times.

inputs.name: networkApi
inputs.description: Object providing network operation callbacks for connecting, disconnecting, and messaging peers

error_cases.name: invalid_network_api
error_cases.condition: networkApi is null, undefined, or missing required methods

error_data.message: networkApi must provide getConnectedPeerCount, getConnectedPeerUrls, connectToPeer, disconnectPeer, sendToPeer, and broadcastRaw functions

preconditions: networkApi is an object with all required function properties
networkApi.getConnectedPeerUrls returns an array of strings
postconditions: Two setInterval timers are running: one at DISCOVERY_INTERVAL, one at ROTATION_INTERVAL
Returned function, when called, clears both intervals
No timers remain active after cleanup function is called
side_effects: Starts two setInterval timers that persist until cleanup is called
Timer callbacks invoke networkApi methods (connectToPeer, disconnectPeer, broadcastRaw, etc.)

structured_side_effects.description: Registers two periodic intervals for discovery health check and peer rotation
Timer callbacks invoke networkApi.connectToPeer, disconnectPeer, broadcastRaw, sendToPeer
Logs warnings when peer count drops below MIN_PEERS

invariants: ROTATION_INTERVAL is always 300000 (5 minutes in ms)
DISCOVERY_INTERVAL is always 30000 (30 seconds in ms)
KnownPeersCache internal Map size never exceeds the configured maxSize
Peer exchange messages are plain JSON objects — never padded, TTL-stamped, signed, or encrypted
Peer exchange messages always have a type field starting with 'peer_exchange_'
LRU eviction removes the entry with the lowest lastSeen value when cache is at max capacity
All exported functions use named exports only — no default export
No private keys, secret keys, or decrypted message content are ever logged
requires: Browser runtime with window.location available for getBootstrapNodes default
crypto.getRandomValues() available for secure random index selection
